name: Foundation/health
description: Health check endpoint conformance tests ported from Microsoft.Health.Fhir.Shared.Tests.E2E Rest/HealthTests.cs. Covers GET /health/check returning HTTP 200. The source test's second assertion (that the raw response body contains the substring 'description') cannot be expressed with this engine: the health report is a plain JSON object with no 'resourceType', and the engine's only body-inspection assertions (resource/expression) require a FHIR resource body to convert via the schema-aware FHIRPath evaluator, while there is no raw substring/body-text assertion available. This is approximated by asserting the response Content-Type is application/json (confirming a structured JSON body was returned), and the limitation is documented here rather than silently dropped.

test.name: health/check returns 200 OK
test.description: GET /health/check must return HTTP 200 when the FHIR server has started successfully.

test.name: health/check returns a JSON body (approximates the 'contains description' check)
test.description: The original test asserts the raw response text contains the substring 'description'. This engine cannot inspect non-FHIR response bodies (see file description), so this is approximated by asserting the response is HTTP 200 with a Content-Type of application/json, confirming a structured health report body was returned.

operation.description: Request the health check endpoint again for an independent content-type check

assert.description: Health check response must be JSON (approximation for the unassertable 'description' substring check)
